title: Swap-hit picker: action row (Use selected / Kit default) hidden behind the browser; swapping a hit is impossible
summary: swap_panel adds two dk.row()s directly to the pushed panel, which has no flex-column layout, so both rows position at (0,0) and overlap. On-device child dump: child0 (top action row) 1024x64 @ (0,0) and child1 (packs+hits body) 1024x448 @ (0,0); the opaque body is drawn last and fully occludes the top row. The 'tap a hit to hear it' instruction and the 'Use selected' and 'Kit default' buttons are therefore invisible AND unclickable (taps land on the body underneath). A user can browse packs and audition hits but has no way to commit the swap or reset to the kit default — only Back (cancel) works, so the Swap-hit feature is non-functional.
suggested_fix: Give swap_panel's parent a vertical layout before adding rows: either set parent.set_flex_flow(lv.FLEX_FLOW.COLUMN)+pad_row (like the other panels that build on a dk.scroll_col), or set explicit positions (top row y=0 h=64; body y=72, h=H-80). Mirror the pattern sound_panel/kit_panel already use. Verify against a screenshot that 'Use selected' and 'Kit default' are visible and tappable.
verification: VERIFIED END-TO-END on-device. Geometry: action row 1024x64 @ (0,0), body @ (0,72) — no overlap (v2_x1a). Packs column lists 42 packs and actually scrolls (scroll_y 0->800). Tapping a pack populated 22 hits; tapping a hit highlighted it ACCENT and set swap_sel='cr78/bongo_l' (v2_x1b); 'Use selected' committed — the Pads card then showed 'bongo_l *' and cfg carried hit_swaps {'36':'cr78/bongo_l'} (v2_x1c). 'Kit default' path restored (hit_swaps emptied; key absent from disk afterwards).

title: Pad editor sliders (Tune/Decay/Level/Snap) have no numeric readouts
summary: In the per-pad sound editor the four sliders show only a left-side label and the knob position — no current value or unit. This is inconsistent with the Sound editor and FX editor, whose sliders all show a live value readout (e.g. '90 %', '28 %'). You cannot tell the current Tune (semitones), Decay/Level/Snap %, or set a precise value; you are tuning blind.
suggested_fix: Add a value label per slider (right-aligned, updated in the VALUE_CHANGED cb) formatted per param, matching the curated/FX sliders' readout style (green mono value + unit). Cheap: one label per row, updated in the existing cb.
verification: VERIFIED (v2_x1c_after_use.png): teal numeric readouts on all four sliders (Tune '0', Decay/Level/Snap '100%'). Readout contrast on the SURFACE2 row is adequate though not generous — acceptable.

title: Kit picker (76 kits) has no search and no sampled/synth grouping; every synth kit carries a repetitive '(synth)' suffix
summary: The Kit picker is a flat list of full-width 64px rows — only ~7 of 76 kits fit per screen (~11 screens of scrolling). Unlike the Patch picker, it has NO search field. There is no divider at the 7-sampled -> 69-synth boundary (Percussion is immediately followed by 'Acoustic (synth)'), and every synth kit is suffixed '(synth)' with heavy variant fan-out ('Acoustic', 'Acoustic B/C/D', ...), so 69 near-identical names are hard to scan or differentiate.
suggested_fix: Add a search field mirroring the Patch picker (instrument.py already has the pattern). Insert a section header/divider row before the synth kits ('Synthesized') and drop the per-row '(synth)' suffix (the section conveys it). Keep repaint cost down by hiding rows rather than rebuilding.
verification: VERIFIED: 'Search kits' field on top (v2_x3a); 'Synthesized' section header at the sampled/synth boundary with '(synth)' suffixes gone (v2_x3b); live filter works — typing '909' filtered to 6 TR-909 kits with the current kit highlighted ACCENT (v2_x3c). Hide-don't-rebuild filtering keeps repaints cheap. Nit: the section header label is small/MUTED — subtle, but it does its job.

title: Disabled controls and favorite stars render muddy olive/khaki in RGB332 (review-8 R-1 still present)
summary: Two off-palette color problems from the 8-bit (RGB332) display: (1) Files' Run/Edit/Delete + the whole action bar read olive/khaki when nothing is selected — dimmed with set_style_opa(102) (40%) over the surface; GREEN/ACCENT at 40% quantize to a muddy olive. (2) Favorite stars used on_color ORANGE=(228,132,44), which quantizes to a muddy olive and sits near-isoluminant with the empty MUTED star — you can't tell a starred patch from an unstarred one.
suggested_fix: Files half, next attempt: the deployed flat-color fix did not change the render because LVGL's theme applies a DISABLED-state COLOR FILTER (style_color_filter) ON TOP of any bg/text color you set. Either clear it — b.set_style_color_filter_opa(0, lv.PART.MAIN | lv.STATE.DISABLED) (and check the label child) — or stop using add_state(DISABLED) for the visual entirely: style the resting look manually (SURFACE2 bg, MUTED text) and gate the action in the callback. Verify against a screenshot, not the source.
verification: STARS FIXED (v2_x4b_stars.png): STAR_ON=(255,224,0) renders bright yellow, STAR_OFF=(72,74,92) clearly dark — starred vs unstarred unmistakable at a glance. FILES NOT FIXED (v2_x4a_files_disabled.png): the action bar renders the SAME olive/khaki as round 1 even though /user/files.py CRC-matches the repo fix — the flat-color approach is deployed but ineffective against the theme's disabled color filter (root cause above).

title: Factory reset is styled identically to Restart (neutral grey) despite being the most destructive action
summary: In Settings > Power, 'Restart' and 'Factory reset' were the same SURFACE2 grey button. Factory reset wipes the device (including Wi-Fi credentials) yet carried zero visual warning weight; the most prominent button in Settings was instead the bright-purple 'Upgrade'.
suggested_fix: Give Factory reset a destructive treatment (RED text/border) distinct from Restart; tone down Upgrade.
verification: VERIFIED (v2_x5_settings.png): Factory reset now has a 2px RED border + RED label, clearly distinct from the grey Restart; Upgrade is demoted to the same SURFACE2 as Set time/Calibrate. Minor residual: the red-on-grey label renders a touch dark in RGB332 (legible but not crisp); the two-tap arm still goes solid red. Acceptable.

title: Center device chip reads as a progress bar, not a tappable device selector
summary: The top-bar center chip ('Tulip 10/32') carried a dark, near-full-width inset track along its bottom; at idle it read as a progress/loading bar stuck near 100% rather than a status+nav chip.
suggested_fix: Slim, low-contrast meter strip; hide the heavy track at idle.
verification: VERIFIED (v2_x6_home_chip.png): the dark near-full inset track is gone; the meter is now a slim low-contrast strip along the chip's bottom edge, near-invisible at idle. The progress-bar misread is resolved. (Chip background remains bright SURFACE2 — acceptable.)

title: Pad editor 'Swap >' button has no contrast against its own row
summary: The hit-name row is SURFACE2 and the 'Swap >' button on it was also SURFACE2, so the button barely read as a button.
suggested_fix: ACCENT background so it stands off the row.
verification: VERIFIED (v2_x1c_after_use.png): Swap > is ACCENT blue, clearly a button.

title: Slider accent colors are inconsistent with no semantic system
summary: Sliders use different fills with no consistent meaning: Volume=green, Brightness=orange (Settings); Member channels=blue, Pitch bend=orange (MPE); Sound/FX=teal; pad sliders=blue; Voices=green. Orange elsewhere signals 'hot' (chip meter >=70%), so using it for ordinary values is a semantic smell.

location.symbol: slider() default color + per-call color args

suggested_fix: Standardize normal-value sliders on one accent (teal or green). Reserve orange/red for warning/over-threshold states.
verification: Deferred by design this round (style-system pass across a dozen call sites). Still open. The SETTINGS-TABS.md redesign is a natural vehicle for the Settings instances (its spec standardizes both Settings sliders on teal).

title: Welcome onboarding shows an orphaned '< Back' button
summary: The first-boot Welcome screen rendered a '< Back' button top-left with nowhere valid to go back to.
suggested_fix: Suppress the standalone Back button on Welcome.
verification: VERIFIED (v2_x9_welcome.png): no Back button, checked via the same tulip.run('welcome') launch path that showed it in round 1.

title: Welcome step cards have title at the top and subtitle at the bottom with an empty middle
summary: Each 300x150 step card pinned the title to the top edge and the subtitle to the bottom edge, leaving a large empty middle.
suggested_fix: Group title+subtitle together near the top.
verification: VERIFIED (v2_x9_welcome.png): title and subtitle now grouped near the top of each card.

title: Double-space artifacts in copy
summary: Doubled spaces in the home/rack subtitle ('ch1  A11 Brass Set 1'), the DX7 patch name ('BRASS  1'), and nav chevrons ('  >').
verification: VERIFIED (v2_x6_home_chip.png, v2_x11_editor.png): subtitle now 'ch1 A11 Brass Set 1'; chevrons single-spaced ('Juno-6 >', 'Browse >', 'Edit >'). DX7 'BRASS  1' is upstream patch-table DATA, not UI copy — accepted as-is. The home footer's wide 'name  sound   |   vol' separators are deliberate visual spacing, not the stutter this finding targeted.

title: Submenu tile colors don't differentiate items (two greens adjacent)
summary: In System, Files and Settings were both green; in Apps, Editor and Wordpad were both green.
suggested_fix: Distinct hues per tile; red reserved for Reset.
verification: VERIFIED (v2_x12_system.png, v2_x12_apps.png): Files is teal (vs Settings green) in System; Wordpad is teal (vs Editor green) in Apps. No adjacent duplicate hues; red stays reserved for Reset.

title: Recurring littlefs 'Corrupted dir pair' console error during /user operations
summary: Round-1 crawl: the console repeatedly emitted 'lib/littlefs/lfs2.c:1346:error: Corrupted dir pair at {0x1, 0x0}' on /user reads/writes. Non-fatal (screenshots and config reads kept working) but a recurring FS-corruption warning on the user partition is a robustness flag. Firmware/FS, not deck UI.
evidence: (console logs during s07, s16, s21, s26 captures)
suggested_fix: Investigate /user littlefs health; confirm it is not progressive corruption. Route to firmware.
verification: Routed to firmware per coordinator. The error did not recur during round-2's /user operations; the round-2 WDT crash (X-14) is likely the same flash-subsystem neighborhood.

title: Interrupt-WDT reboot (CPU1) triggered by a config flash write followed immediately by heavy panel navigation
summary: Reproduced live during round-2 verification: deckcfg.toggle_favorite() (a config flash write) followed within ~1s by reset_to_root() + a Settings panel push caused 'Guru Meditation Error: Core 1 panic'ed (Interrupt wdt timeout on CPU1)' and a full reboot (~40s outage; DECKLOG confirms reset_cause=WDT). Core 1 was in ISR context; the backtrace goes through the flash/cache path (ELF SHA e8af2da56, PC 0x40056f5c, EPC4 0x40056f5c). Third WDT of the night in this class — the coordinator reported two earlier during flash writes. The flash-fence work (b2a25d9a/eb1dd2bc) covers AMY PCM renders vs flash writes, but this trace says UI work racing a config write can still starve CPU1's interrupt watchdog. User impact: a performer toggling a favorite (or any config write) and immediately navigating can reboot the deck mid-session.
evidence: (serial capture, round-2 session: full Guru Meditation register dump + backtrace; DECKLOG 'boot: reset cause WDT')

location.symbol: _write scheduling vs CPU1 ISR during the cache-disabled flash window

suggested_fix: Firmware-side: extend the flash-fence discipline to cover UI/CPU load during the write window. Deck-side mitigation worth trying now: in deckcfg._write, defer the write while a panel build/navigation is in flight (not only while audio is hot), and avoid triggering navigation in the same tick as a flush. Needs a firmware owner; keep the serial dump attached to this finding.
